conditions: Eating disorder (in recovery; restrictive eating history)
medications: Hydroxyzine 25 mg as needed for anxiety/sleep
Naproxen 500 mg as needed for pain/inflammation
Topical diclofenac gel for shoulder pain
health.mental_health: In recovery from an eating disorder and managing anxiety/rumination. Being closeted in a conservative circle increases hypervigilance and stress, especially around family and coworkers; uses therapy and structured meals to avoid relapse.
recent_visits: Orthopedist follow-up for ACL reconstruction clearance and knee stability testing (last month)
Physical therapy sessions 2x/week for knee strengthening and gait work (ongoing)
Sports medicine visit for shoulder pain and home exercise plan (6 weeks ago)
Outpatient therapy session focused on eating disorder recovery and anxiety (weekly)
Nutritionist check-in for meal plan adjustments (two weeks ago)

finance.debt: About $9,500 in student loan debt from community college and ~$2,300 on a credit card used during surgery recovery when hours were reduced; actively paying down and avoids family knowing the extent of it.
recent_transactions: $72.18 grocery run focused on recovery-friendly staples (protein, produce)
$46.50 rideshare to an evening therapy appointment to avoid being seen driving there
$28.00 gift purchase for a coworker’s baby shower

relationships: Parents (live in the metroplex; conservative and active in church; she filters what she shares)
Younger brother (19; easier to talk to but she’s still not out to him)
Best friend: Marisol Vega (friend from high school; knows about her recovery and is one of the few she’s partially open with)
Close coworker: Jenna Park (front desk agent; supportive but Isabella avoids personal topics at work)
Quiet dating connection: 'Kate' (met through a queer-friendly meetup in Oak Lawn; kept discreet)
social.social_media_activity: Keeps Instagram mostly neutral (work-friendly posts, Dallas spots, fitness/rehab progress without specifics). Uses a private account with a small circle for more personal updates; avoids tagging locations at LGBTQ+ venues.
private_conversations: Texting Marisol about fear of being outed and strategies for navigating family gatherings
Therapy-related check-ins about eating disorder urges and body image triggers
Discreet messages with Kate about where to meet without running into coworkers or family friends
Discussing whether to transfer to a different hotel to feel safer being herself

schedule.work_hours: Rotating shifts, typically 40–45 hours/week: 7am–3pm or 3pm–11pm; occasional 11pm–7am coverage when short-staffed. Works most weekends; two weekdays off that vary.
regular_appointments: Physical therapy: Tuesdays and Thursdays at 6:30pm
Individual therapy: Wednesdays at 8:00pm (telehealth from her car or bedroom for privacy)
upcoming_events: Orthopedist re-evaluation for return-to-sport guidance (next Friday morning)
Coworker baby shower at a restaurant in Addison (next Sunday afternoon)
Family dinner for her mom’s birthday (in two weeks; she’s anxious about questions about dating)
Local LGBTQ+ book club meeting in Oak Lawn (next Thursday evening; attending discreetly)
schedule.daily_routine: Up early on day shifts for a short rehab routine, packs structured snacks to stay consistent with recovery, works the desk managing escalations and staffing, then either PT/therapy in the evening or a quiet night in with stretching and meal prep.

professional.company: Midscale business hotel near Dallas Love Field (franchise of a national brand)
projects: Reducing guest complaint response time and standardizing escalation notes
Training two new front desk hires on PMS workflows and night audit handoffs
Updating shift checklists (cash handling, incident logs, and ADA room blocks)
Coordinating with housekeeping to improve turnaround on high-occupancy weekends
professional.performance_issues: Generally strong performance, but occasional fatigue and concentration dips after late therapy/telehealth nights; avoids discussing the reason for appointments and sometimes requests last-minute shift swaps, which draws questions she tries to deflect.
workplace_relationships: Front office manager (Derek Nguyen): supportive but expects flexibility with staffing gaps
Night auditor (Lance Whitaker): reliable but gossipy; she is careful about personal details
Housekeeping supervisor (Rosa Martinez): good working rapport; relies on Isabella for room block coordination
A few coworkers make casual conservative comments; she stays neutral to avoid attention

hobbies: Rehab-friendly gym routines and stationary biking
Reading romance and contemporary fiction (often via library app)
Coffee shop journaling in quieter neighborhoods
Trying new Dallas food spots with a small trusted circle
habits: Meal planning and setting reminders to eat regular snacks (recovery support)
Tracking PT exercises and pain levels in a notes app
Keeping a private locked folder for therapy worksheets and a discreet contact list
Avoiding certain neighborhoods/venues where she might run into family friends
Using a separate email for LGBTQ+ groups and event RSVPs
lifestyle.living_situation: Shares a two-bedroom apartment in North Dallas with a roommate (Alyssa Kline, 23). Presents her social life as mostly work-focused to avoid roommate gossip reaching mutual acquaintances; keeps dating and queer-community involvement low-profile.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: No strict restrictions; follows a structured, balanced approach for eating-disorder recovery (regular meals, adequate protein/carbs). Limits alcohol and caffeine when anxiety or sleep issues flare; avoids aggressive dieting talk.
